hero.title: Windrose
hero.description: Windrose is a pirate survival adventure on Steam focused on ship combat, base building, open-world exploration, bosses, and co-op progression across land and sea.

gameFeature.title: Windrose Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Explore surreal biomes, fuse items with Apotheosis, and survive strange creatures in this dreamlike voxel sandbox on Steam.

lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Windrose Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Windrose gets much smoother once your opening route is structured. Secure coastal materials, establish a real starter camp, stabilize healing and food, then push caves, quests, and ship progression.

steps.title: Scavenge the beach before you chase combat
steps.description: Start on the shoreline for safe, efficient gathering. This route gives you core materials for tools and shelter without forcing risky early fights.

steps.title: Set a real day-one camp, not just a campfire
steps.description: Build a functional camp with respawn, crafting, cooking, and storage from the start so early deaths and inventory pressure do not reset momentum.

steps.title: Fight like a stamina game, not a shooter
steps.description: Early combat rewards blocks, parries, dodges, and posture breaks. Firearms are best used as finishers after creating a safe opening.

steps.title: Use map markers and bells to cut runback time
steps.description: Track resources and routes with markers, unlock your first bell quickly, and place travel points near dungeon entrances to remove repeated downtime.

steps.title: Build forward camps before bosses and ruins
steps.description: A small forward camp turns difficult content into efficient retries. Keep a tent outside hard zones to reset quickly after wipe or unload.

steps.title: Stabilize land progression before full sea progression
steps.description: Your biggest early gains come from base quality, survivability, and travel setup on land. Sailing value rises once those systems are stable.

quickTips: Prioritize wood, plant fiber, nails, scallop shells, loose stone, and riverbank clay in the opening route.
Starter camp core: bonfire, workbench, cooking fire, bed, and tent.
Carry at least 10 bandages and two food types before longer combat routes.
Bandages heal over time, so create space first and then recover.
Place bells outside dungeons so return trips stay short and repeatable.
Before Thomas Richards, prepare a weapon, firearm, food, potions, bandages, and a full armor set.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Windrose Release Date
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Windrose is already live in Early Access. The useful release view is launch timing, current build status, and how the path toward 1.0 is developing.

cards.name: 2026-03-23 - Pre-launch update
cards.description: The demo period remained active until Early Access while the team outlined launch-quality improvements from large player feedback batches.

cards.name: 2026-04-09 - Release date confirmed
cards.description: The studio confirmed April 14, 2026 for Early Access launch with a base price of $29.99 and an announced Supporter Bundle.

cards.name: 2026-04-14 - Early Access live
cards.description: Windrose launched on Steam and Epic Games Store with optional co-op, a long-form main story, multiple biomes, and playable ship progression.

cards.name: 2026-04-17 - Launch-week hotfix
cards.description: Hotfix 0.10.0.2.54 improved online stability, outdated-server checks, invite code support, and boss scaling behavior for larger co-op groups.

cards.name: Late 2027 to Late 2028 - Estimated 1.0 window
cards.description: Current Early Access planning points to a 1.5-2.5 year development window from launch, with major content growth targeted for full release.

milestones: Current platform status: Steam + Epic Games Store
Current 1.0 target window: Late 2027 to Late 2028

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Windrose Multiplayer Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Windrose co-op is straightforward once hosting, invite flow, world settings, and dedicated server options are mapped clearly.

items.name: Online co-op only
items.description: Windrose does not use local split-screen. Friends join from separate PCs online, and solo offline play is still available.

items.name: Convert a solo world into a co-op world
items.description: From Play -> Host a game, you can open an existing save, generate an invite code, and continue progression with friends in the same world.

items.name: Invite-code join flow
items.description: Players join through Connect to Server with an invite code. Once joined, the server can be saved for faster re-entry.

items.name: Configurable world rules for shared runs
items.description: Hosts can tune shared quest progression, enemy scaling behavior, and password-protected access to match the group setup.

items.name: Player cap up to 8, comfort range around 4
items.description: Dedicated server configs can support up to 8 players, while smaller crews are usually smoother for consistency and performance.

items.name: Dedicated servers for always-on worlds
items.description: The official dedicated server app keeps worlds online when hosts are away, with invite code output and version matching as core operations.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Windrose Best Stats and Talents
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Windrose early progression is strongest when you commit to one damage lane, secure stamina and health first, and choose talents that keep boss attempts stable.

solutions.name: Do not split your damage stats
solutions.description: Pick one damage lane and commit. Early split investment reduces combat efficiency more than a focused build with modest gear.

solutions.name: Build stamina and health before peak damage
solutions.description: The safest opener invests in Endurance and Vitality first, then scales the damage stat that matches your primary weapon family.

solutions.name: Understand support stats before spending
solutions.description: Mastery, Vitality, and Endurance each solve different combat pressure. Knowing their exact value prevents weak early allocation.

solutions.name: Prioritize survival talents early
solutions.description: Stamina, health, and damage resistance talents reduce wipe chains and make early caves and first bosses much more manageable.

solutions.name: Match your talent tree to your playstyle
solutions.description: Choose one tree based on fight style and commit long enough to feel the power curve, instead of scattering points across unrelated nodes.

managementTips: Strength scales clubs and halberds; Agility scales sabers, greatswords, and blunderbusses; Precision scales rapiers, pistols, and muskets.
Mastery grants +0.5% critical chance per point.
Just a Flesh Wound provides 6% / 9% / 12% melee damage resistance.
Too Angry to Die revives you at 30% health after a killing blow.
Fencer focuses fast melee DPS, Crusher focuses bruiser pressure, Marksman focuses ranged output, and Defender adds universal survivability.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Windrose Builds and Classes
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Windrose does not lock you into one class, but talent trees still reward clear identities: fast melee, bruiser pressure, ranged output, or a survival-first path.

weaponDirection: Cutlass or Razor for an Agility route
Sturdy Rapier into Rapier of a Thousand Cuts for a Precision route
armorDirection: Flibustier's Attire for a one-handed melee setup
Buccaneer 2-piece plus Marksman's Rig 2-piece for a low-stamina hybrid dodge setup
paths.playPattern: Play around short punish windows, dodge through attacks instead of away from them, and keep stamina for one more evasive move.
bestFor: Boss fights once patterns are learned
Players who want high melee damage pace

weaponDirection: Plague Halberd or Executioner as the heavy follow-up path
armorDirection: Pikeman's Armor for a pure two-handed identity
Pikeman 2-piece plus Conquistador 2-piece for a safe early-mid front line mix
paths.playPattern: Use reach, stagger, and posture pressure to control packs, then cash in larger punish windows with halberd swings.
bestFor: Players who want safer crowd control than Fencer

weaponDirection: Pistol into Reliable Musket for a stable start
Reliable Pistol or Drake's Double-Barreled Pistol as strong sidearms
Blunderbuss when you can support ammo usage
armorDirection: Marksman's Rig 4-piece for straight ranged damage
Mix survival talents if you want a safer solo version
paths.playPattern: Kite, shoot, reposition, and spend ammunition on guaranteed windows instead of panic firing.
bestFor: Players who want a simple stat plan

weaponDirection: Most often paired with Marksman or Crusher instead of played alone
armorDirection: Conquistador 2-piece plus Tracker's Leathers 2-piece for healing-heavy tanking
Conquistador 2-piece plus Pikeman 2-piece for early-mid durability
paths.playPattern: Trade speed for forgiveness, survive mistakes that end other builds, and keep uptime through durability.
bestFor: Players learning routes or higher difficulties

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Windrose Best Weapons
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: The Windrose launch meta favors quick Agility and Precision options. Strength lines still hit hard, but they ask for better spacing and stamina discipline.

progressionExamples: Mid game: Rapier of a Thousand Cuts
Endgame: Rapier of a Thousand Cuts remains a premier boss weapon
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: Low commitment and easy to keep active
weaponTiers.bossValue: Excellent single-target damage with safe punish timing
weaponTiers.quickTake: Rapier is one of the cleanest launch answers to bosses because it stacks bleed fast and keeps pressure high while moving.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Razor remains one of the safest all-round picks
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: Low strain and forgiving for continuous pressure
weaponTiers.bossValue: Strong for repeated safe hits and crowd control between fights
weaponTiers.quickTake: Saber is the most stable all-round melee family at launch, with Razor adding crit value and better perfect block windows.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Dragon's Breath stays lethal at close range
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: Handling is manageable, but ammo and positioning are the real tax
weaponTiers.bossValue: Very high burst if you hold close range safely
weaponTiers.quickTake: Blunderbuss remains a boss killer when your ammo economy is stable.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Drake's remains valuable for Vulnerability support
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: Easy to weave into mobile play
weaponTiers.bossValue: Great ranged support for Precision builds
weaponTiers.quickTake: Pistol is the easiest sidearm family to slot into real fights and keeps ranged pressure flexible.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Plague Halberd remains the standout Strength spearhead
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: Higher commitment than fast one-hand options
weaponTiers.bossValue: Very strong when punish windows are known
weaponTiers.quickTake: Halberd is the practical Strength route and scales well once you can trigger its special windows consistently.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Stalwart Greatsword is the steadier carry
weaponTiers.bossValue: Good damage when you read recovery windows
weaponTiers.quickTake: Greatsword is the heavier Agility option and rewards disciplined timing.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Swamp Creature's Tooth for AoE distortion bursts
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: Slow and costly if positioning is loose
weaponTiers.bossValue: High per-hit payoff, but less forgiving than halberd
weaponTiers.quickTake: Club can still hit hard, but current launch balance makes it less consistent than faster families.

progressionExamples: Endgame: Reliable Musket still outperforms baseline muskets
weaponTiers.staminaProfile: The issue is reload tempo and recovery pacing, not stamina
weaponTiers.bossValue: Usable, but usually weaker than pistol or blunderbuss unless built around
weaponTiers.quickTake: Most muskets are slow in the current meta, with Reliable Musket as the clear exception.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Windrose Ship Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Ship progression is the core fantasy loop once you leave tutorial islands. The practical route is Boat to Ketch to Brig to Frigate, with the Wharf powering every upgrade step.

shipStages.howToUnlock: Automatic after the first main quest and summonable at shorelines with K
shipStages.role: Short-range starter dinghy for travel only
shipStages.keyStats: About 10 knots with no real combat value
shipStages.bestPick: Use it only for early story steps
shipStages.whenToUpgrade: Replace it as soon as Need A Bigger Boat opens

shipStages.howToUnlock: Finish Need A Bigger Boat and Rescuing the Crew, repair the stranded ship, then tow it out with rescued crew
shipStages.role: First real ship for Coastal Jungle scouting, low-risk trade runs, and first naval fights
shipStages.bestPick: Stock Ketch is the cleanest early balance pick
shipStages.whenToUpgrade: Upgrade at the Wharf through Coastal Jungle, then transition once Brig plans are available

shipStages.howToUnlock: Buy the design from the Brethren Provisioner at Reputation Level 2 and build at the Wharf
shipStages.role: Mid-game warship for harder naval combat and longer encounters
shipStages.keyStats: Stock Brig: 70,000 HP, 20kn, 6 x 12lb; Blackbeard Brig: 50,000 HP, 22kn, 6 x 12lb or 24lb
shipStages.bestPick: Blackbeard Brig for speed and 24-pounders; Stock Brig for steadier hull value
shipStages.whenToUpgrade: At this tier, cannon choice, hull mods, and repair-kit timing decide most fights

shipStages.howToUnlock: Buy the design from the Brethren Provisioner at Reputation Level 4 and build at the Wharf
shipStages.role: Late-game flagship for boss patrols, fleet fights, and faction pressure
shipStages.keyStats: Stock Frigate: 160,000 HP, 18kn, 12 x 24lb; Blackbeard Frigate: 110,000 HP, 20kn, 12 x 24lb or 36lb
shipStages.bestPick: Blackbeard Frigate is the aggressive endgame pick; Brethren Frigate is the tankiest hull
shipStages.whenToUpgrade: Worth the material grind once you are ready for full naval loadouts and harder encounters

shipStages.howToUnlock: After your first real ship, place a Wharf near water and craft ship gear at the Shipwright's Workshop
shipStages.keyStats: Each real ship has four gear slots: Cannons, Hull Modification, Boarding Party Gear, and Naval Tactics
shipStages.bestPick: Perfectly Ordered cannons, Hull Bracing: Keelhold, and Naval Tactics III: Stretch The Supply are top current priorities
shipStages.whenToUpgrade: Start from Ketch tier; by Brig and Frigate tier these upgrades shape every naval battle

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Windrose Boss Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: These four encounters define launch progression from Coastal Jungle into late Cursed Swamps. Prep first, learn patterns second, and push damage last.

bosses.where: Coastal Jungle during Revenge Is Best Served Cold
prep: Upgrade weapon, ranged weapon, and all armor pieces to level 5
Run two food buffs plus the Rested buff
Bring Healing Potions, bullets, and gunpowder
Use a Blunderbuss if your ammo economy supports it
A solid two-piece armor mix makes the fight steadier
bosses.winningPlan: Dodge red-glint moves, break shields, take stagger windows, and chip down instead of forcing extra hits.
rewards: Opens the next land progression tier

bosses.where: Foothills during Needle in a Haystack
prep: Max gear to level 10
Ascend weapons to Epic if damage windows still feel tight
Bring a full stack of Healing Potions and strong food buffs
Rapier of a Thousand Cuts plus a pistol is the cleanest loadout
Boar Whistle support from Truffle helps stabilize the encounter
bosses.winningPlan: Maintain bleed stacks, punish after melee strings, use ranged shots during summon windows, and avoid greedy extra swings.

prep: Bring 15-20 healing food portions
Set a tent at the dungeon entrance
Bring Coffee or another Endurance dish
Fast weapons such as Saber or Rapier outperform slow heavies
Rapier of a Thousand Cuts is an excellent single-target pick
Too Angry to Die is premium insurance for final phase mistakes
attackPatterns: Phase 2: tracking projectiles and add pressure
Phase 3: faster strings plus a tight AoE slam
Phase transitions attack immediately without pause
bosses.winningPlan: Dodge through cutlass arcs, strafe against projectiles, clear adds before they stack, and keep the safest decisions for the final 30 percent.

prep: Bring gear tier 11-15 with a fully upgraded Rare or Epic loadout
Use crude-damage weapons such as halberds, clubs, or maces and keep a pierce backup
Bring Elixir of Pain Relief
Bring Great Healing Potions
Stack damage food plus long-duration HP and stamina recipes
Co-op helps because one player can draw aggression while others deal damage
attackPatterns: Rooted arena control instead of constant boss movement
Area plague clouds that apply damage over time
bosses.winningPlan: Manage space around her rooted position, clear thralls before they stack, avoid plague cloud uptime, and lean on crude-damage pressure through the long fight.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Windrose Crafting Stations
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.subtitle: Build the stations that actually unlock your next tier.
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: In Windrose, station progression comes from add-ons more than from one flat recipe list. If a craft feels blocked, the answer is usually a missing attachment, not just more raw materials.

stations.label: Workbench
unlockFocus: Stone tools, Rope, Nails, Repair Kit, and Lamp crafting
Fast Travel Bell once copper progression is online
Bag progression through higher Workbench levels

upgrades.name: Sawhorse
whatItAdds: Workbench Level 2
Sailor Backpack and other Level 2 Workbench recipes

upgrades.name: Toolbox
whatItAdds: Workbench Level 3
Bosun Backpack, Millstone Parts, Timber, Tarred Planks, and other midgame utilities

stations.bestUse: Rush Sawhorse before your first serious sailing loop so inventory and utility recipes stop bottlenecking you.

stations.label: Cooking Fire
unlockFocus: Early sustain food and your first reliable meal chain
Upgrade line recipes include Skewered Meat, Bacon and Eggs, Chowder, and Seafood Platter
Long cave runs and sea trips feel much safer once this line is upgraded

upgrades.name: Cutting Table
whatItAdds: Expanded meal prep line
Your first real jump in cooking depth

upgrades.name: Cookware Shelf
whatItAdds: Higher-tier cooking recipes
A much better food economy for midgame travel

stations.bestUse: Upgrade this right after your Workbench if food is forcing you to cut trips short.

stations.label: Weaponsmith Workshop
unlockFocus: Club, Cutlass, Crusher, pistols, muskets, and other early weapons
Ship gear branch through the Shipwright's Workshop path

upgrades.name: Shipwright's Workshop
whatItAdds: Ship equipment and upgrades
Cannons, hull paths, and naval support progression

upgrades.name: Anvil
whatItAdds: Expanded weapons and ship fittings line
A key second step for serious combat scaling

upgrades.name: Bellows
whatItAdds: Late swamp-tier smithing expansion
A broader endgame weapon and ship recipe pool

stations.bestUse: Build this as soon as copper smelting is stable, then add Shipwright's Workshop before cannon-heavy naval fights.

stations.label: Alchemy Table
unlockFocus: Minor Healing Potion, Homeward Journey, Alchemical Base, and early elixirs
Potion support for hard fights and long expeditions
Starts the full healing and buff economy

upgrades.name: Stove and Pot
whatItAdds: The first real potion branch
Healing and utility brews after Misty Orchid discovery

upgrades.name: Reagent Table
whatItAdds: Expanded elixir line
Better combat consumables and deeper alchemy progression

stations.bestUse: Set this up once combat damage and cave exploration start burning through your basic healing stock.

stations.label: Armor and Clothing Workshop
unlockFocus: Early armor sets and cloth or leather equipment
Progression for boots, gloves, jackets, and other worn gear
A major defensive jump once its add-ons are online

upgrades.name: Material Rack
whatItAdds: A much larger advanced gear pool
Quartermaster-tier backpack and swamp-linked armor progression

upgrades.name: Shoemaker's Bench
whatItAdds: Boot and footwear expansion
A stronger Foothills-era armor and mobility line

stations.bestUse: Build it when Rough Hide is steady, then add Shoemaker's Bench once flax and tanned leather stop feeling scarce.

stations.label: Jewelery Table
unlockFocus: Accessory upgrades that start with silver and scale into swamp metals
A late-midgame power layer once your core gear is already stable

upgrades.name: Jeweler's Bench
whatItAdds: Major ring tiers
The real payoff for accessory-focused builds

stations.bestUse: Treat this as a luxury station until travel, weapons, armor, and farming are already under control.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Windrose Resources Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.subtitle: Track the materials that gate tools, travel, ships, and better gear.
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The real resource question in Windrose is not just where a node spawns, but what chain it unlocks next. These are the materials players keep searching because one missing row can stall an entire run.

resources.foundIn: Palm trees, Ficus trees, and loose beach debris around the Coastal Jungle.
resources.needs: Axe for tree farming. Loose logs can be picked up without one.
resources.usedFor: Every early station chain, Fast Travel Points, Repair Kits, and general base building.
resources.routeTip: Beach pickups solve the tutorial. Ficus chopping is what actually scales your wood income.

resources.foundIn: Muddy rock patches on open ground, usually outside caves.
resources.usedFor: Clay Bottles, Charcoal Kilns, and a large share of early stations.
resources.routeTip: A single deposit gives roughly 50-60 clay, so a base near two deposits saves a lot of return trips.

resources.needs: Stone Pickaxe at minimum, plus a lamp or torches for safer cave runs.
resources.usedFor: Copper Ingots, then tools, bells, nails, and early weapons.
resources.routeTip: Copper is much better mined in batches. Four ore plus charcoal smelts into one ingot.

resources.foundIn: Boars and Sows in the Coastal Jungle, then Wolves and Goats in the Foothills.
resources.needs: Combat farming, or Merchant Contracts once you want bulk restocks.
resources.usedFor: Armor, backpacks, and a long list of midgame crafts.
resources.routeTip: If raw hunting starts feeling slow, Merchant Contracts become the clean bulk-buy solution.

resources.foundIn: Refilled at the Workbench instead of gathered as a direct world node.
resources.needs: 3 Animal Fat and an Empty Lamp.
resources.usedFor: Cave runs, ruin routes, and any serious copper or treasure farming session.
resources.routeTip: Savage or Charging Boars can drop multiple chunks of fat, which makes lamp refills much less annoying.

resources.foundIn: Divi-Divi trees and stumps throughout the Foothills.
resources.usedFor: Toolbox, Timber, advanced tools, and many midgame ship or station recipes.
resources.routeTip: Your first big Foothills wood spike should go into tooling and ship support, not decoration.

resources.foundIn: The same Divi-Divi trees and stumps that provide Hardwood in the Foothills.
resources.usedFor: Tannin, Tanned Leather, and the upgraded leather chain.
resources.routeTip: Bark is the quiet bottleneck behind good boots, backpacks, and armor upgrades.

resources.foundIn: Small purple plant clusters throughout the Foothills.
resources.needs: No tool to pick up; a Spinning Wheel to turn it into Linen Fabric.
resources.usedFor: Linen Fabric, Shoemaker's Bench, and stronger armor or backpack progression.
resources.routeTip: Your first pickup teaches both the Spinning Wheel and the Shoemaker's Bench.

resources.foundIn: Yellow-streaked rocky outcroppings in the Foothills, with additional nodes sometimes appearing in the Coastal Jungle.
resources.needs: Iron Pickaxe for the full mining route.
resources.routeTip: Even if you spot sulfur early, your real sulfur economy starts only after Iron Pickaxe progression.

resources.foundIn: Fields around Ancient Village landmarks in the Foothills.
resources.usedFor: Millstone Parts, Millstones, Cornmeal, and several better food recipes.
resources.routeTip: The first Corn pickup is the moment homemade gunpowder becomes a realistic plan.

resources.foundIn: Ancient Farm landmarks in the Foothills and flowerbed ruins in the Cursed Swamps.
resources.needs: Collection in the field, then Seedbeds back at base.
resources.usedFor: Permanent farming lines for flax, food crops, and swamp plants.
resources.routeTip: Once you have this, stop treating every crop as a dedicated field trip.

resources.foundIn: Loot from pirate camps, ruins, ship caches, and later your own Millstones.
resources.needs: Millstones plus Ash and Sulfur for the crafted route.
resources.usedFor: Firearms, cannon support, and Buccaneer deliveries.
resources.routeTip: Looted powder carries the early game. Millstones take over once Corn and Iron are solved.

resources.foundIn: Ancient Ruins in the Cursed Swamps.
resources.needs: Late-region access and a route that can survive plague zones.
resources.usedFor: Jeweler's Bench, Large Smelting Furnace, and Blacksmith Bellows.
resources.routeTip: This is the swamp material that actually flips your late crafting tier on.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Windrose Map and Biomes
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.subtitle: Read the world by region role, not by one fixed map screenshot.
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Windrose is procedural, so the useful map is a biome logic map. What matters most is what each region unlocks, how hard it hits, and when you should move your base or route onward.

regions.title: How the Windrose map actually works
regions.summary: Your save follows the same regional logic as everyone else's, but the exact island and POI layout is procedural.
keyPoints: 3 current biomes
~30 procedural islands
90+ hand-crafted points of interest
No two saves share the same exact island layout
regions.nextUnlock: A world that rewards route planning more than screenshot memorization.
regions.playerGoal: Use biome roles and service hubs to build your own navigation network.

regions.title: Windrose recommended progression route
regions.summary: The smoothest progression path is tutorial island first, then a real Coastal Jungle base, then a Foothills outpost, then targeted Cursed Swamps runs.
keyPoints: Do not overinvest in the tutorial island
Turn on your first fast travel pair before major ship trips
Move farming and iron-era crafting to the Foothills
Treat the Swamps as a late-resource loop, not your first permanent main base
regions.nextUnlock: Cleaner sailing loops and far fewer painful rebuilds.
regions.playerGoal: Let each region solve one tier of problems before you move your core operations.

regions.title: Windrose Coastal Jungle
regions.summary: This is the first full biome and the place where survival basics turn into real progression.
keyPoints: Copper Ore, Clay, Rough Hide, and Misty Orchid all matter here
Thomas Richards is the region boss
Clearing him unlocks Foothills access and Level 6+ crafting
regions.nextUnlock: Copper tools, fast travel, early armor, and your first serious camp.
regions.playerGoal: Build your first real base here and solve copper, food, and mobility before pushing onward.

regions.title: Windrose Foothills
regions.summary: The Foothills are where Windrose opens its true midgame: iron, cloth, farming, and ship support all start clicking together here.
keyPoints: Foothills Iron Ore, Hardwood, Tree Bark, Flax Fiber, Corn, Sulfur, and Fertile Soil define the biome
Israel Hands is the region boss
Clearing him unlocks Cursed Swamps access and Level 11+ crafting
regions.nextUnlock: Iron tools, Linen Fabric, Millstones, stronger ships, and a real farming economy.
regions.playerGoal: Stand up your iron, cloth, and crop backbone here before you touch the late region seriously.

regions.title: Windrose Cursed Swamps
regions.summary: This is the current final region in Early Access and the part of the game that starts expecting full preparation.
keyPoints: Ancient Scraps, Tar, Plague Wood, Crocodile Hide Piece, and Bromeliad all matter here
Glowing pink plague forest patches are the signature environmental threat
This is the last region available in the current Early Access build
regions.nextUnlock: Late smithing, accessory upgrades, swamp alchemy materials, and the current top crafting tier.
regions.playerGoal: Come here for targeted late-game materials, not for a rushed first foothold.

regions.title: Windrose Tortuga and faction hubs
regions.summary: These islands matter more for services, routing, and reputation than for raw gathering.
keyPoints: Some faction locations include pre-built fast travel points
Tortuga functions as a practical service stop in the midgame sea loop
Bounty Agents, Provisioners, Buyers, and faction quests make these islands worth weaving into your route
regions.nextUnlock: Better sea routing, easier restocking, and smoother faction progression.
regions.playerGoal: Use hubs as anchors on your sailing route instead of trying to force them into your main resource base.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Windrose Fast Travel
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.subtitle: Unlock fast travel before sailing starts wasting whole sessions.
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Windrose does have fast travel early, but it is built as a network you place yourself. Once the Bell-and-Point loop clicks, travel time drops hard.

steps.title: Smelt your first Copper Ingot in Windrose
steps.description: Fast travel unlocks during the early main quest "How My Shore Adventure Began" the moment your first Copper Ingot is made.
details: That discovery unlocks the Fast Travel Bell recipe.
Copper Ore comes from Coastal Jungle caves and has to be smelted first.

steps.title: Get your first Windrose Fast Travel Bell
steps.description: You can either craft the bell directly or loot one before your copper economy feels comfortable.
details: Crafting cost: 10 Copper Ingots and 3 Rope.
Early loot sources include shipwreck debris, beach chests, and the Smuggler's Cache route.

steps.title: Place a Windrose Fast Travel Point on the shoreline
steps.description: The bell does nothing by itself. Open Build Mode, go to Crafting and Utilities, and turn it into a Fast Travel Point.
details: Fast Travel Point cost: 20 Wood and 1 Fast Travel Bell.
Placement rule: coast, beach, or dock only. It cannot be dropped in the middle of an island.

steps.title: Activate the Windrose network with a second point
steps.description: One point is only a marker. Two points are the moment the system starts saving real time.
details: You need at least two Fast Travel Points in different locations.
Two distant points on the same island can activate the network early, even before you spread to other islands.

steps.title: Travel differently on foot and at sea in Windrose
steps.description: Windrose treats land travel and ship travel as two different fast-travel modes.
details: On foot: walk to a Fast Travel Point, then select another point.
On a Ketch: open the map from anywhere at sea and jump to any unlocked point as long as you are not in combat or being chased.

steps.title: Fold pre-built and faction points into your Windrose route
steps.description: Your best network is not built only from your own bases. The world already contains useful anchor points.
details: Some faction bases, including Tortuga-style hubs, can add pre-built fast travel points once visited.
In co-op, map-based fast travel can separate you from your ship and crewmates, so coordinate before jumping.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Windrose System Requirements
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.subtitle: Can your PC run Windrose without bottlenecks?
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Windrose asks for strong desktop hardware even at the minimum tier. The baseline is 16 GB RAM for solo or co-op play, and self-hosting a dedicated server on the same machine needs additional memory headroom.

items.notes: Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system.

items.minimum: Intel Core i7-8700K or AMD Ryzen 7 2700X
items.recommended: Intel Core i7-10700 or AMD Ryzen 7 5800X
items.notes: Official processor examples listed for Windrose.
The 16 GB baseline applies to standard solo and co-op sessions.

items.minimum: NVIDIA GTX 1080 Ti or AMD Radeon RX 6800
items.recommended: NVIDIA RTX 3080 or AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT
items.notes: Official GPU examples used for minimum and recommended tiers.
A 10 GB dedicated VRAM budget keeps both tiers stable.
Windrose uses the same DirectX requirement across both tiers.
An SSD is strongly recommended for load consistency.
Required for online co-op and dedicated-server play.

items.minimum: Add 8 GB on top of the client requirement
items.notes: Running client and server together needs extra memory headroom.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Windrose Dedicated Server Guide
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.subtitle: Set up a Windrose dedicated server from first boot to update flow.
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: The fastest route is the free Windrose Dedicated Server tool on Steam or the WindowsServer build bundled with the game files. Control lives in ServerDescription.json and WorldDescription.json, so the practical flow is install, boot once, then edit generated files while the server is offline.

items.title: Install the Windrose server files
items.description: Install the Windrose Dedicated Server tool from Steam Library > Tools, or copy the WindowsServer build from game files if you use another storefront.
details: Steam route: install the free Windrose Dedicated Server tool.
EGS or Stove route: copy the R5/Builds/WindowsServer folder out of the game directory.
For copied builds, keep the server in a dedicated folder outside the main game install.

items.title: Boot once and collect the invite code
items.description: Run StartServerForeground.bat for the first launch so you can see logs directly, then copy the generated invite code from console output or ServerDescription.json.
details: StartServerForeground.bat is the easiest first-launch method.
WindroseServer.exe can run headless after setup.
Players join through Play > Connect to Server using your invite code.

items.title: Edit Windrose ServerDescription.json
items.description: Use this file to define invite code, password settings, server name, bound world, and max players. Apply edits only while the server is stopped.
details: Key fields: InviteCode, IsPasswordProtected, Password, ServerName, WorldIslandId, MaxPlayerCount.
Invite codes are case-sensitive.
Up to 4 players is usually the smoother baseline even if larger groups are possible.

items.title: Edit Windrose WorldDescription.json
items.description: This file controls world difficulty and scaling. Use presets or custom values for enemy stats, ship damage values, boarding pressure, and co-op balancing.
details: Supports Easy, Medium, Hard, and custom sliders.
Shared quest completion can auto-complete for active party members.
WorldIslandId in server settings must match the selected world file.

items.title: Confirm networking limits and host behavior
items.description: Windrose relies on invite codes and NAT punch-through behavior rather than a fixed-port browser list. Stable UPnP routing improves discovery success.
details: Connections are handled through dynamic NAT punch-through.
UPnP-capable routers reduce join failures.
If discovery fails, remove proxy or VPN layers and retry.

items.title: Keep Windrose client and server versions matched
items.description: After each patch or hotfix, update server files and validate before reopening invites. Version mismatch warnings should be treated as mandatory maintenance.
details: SteamCMD app ID for server updates: 4129620.
Repeat full update flow with force_install_dir, login, and app_update 4129620 validate.
Current admin operations remain primarily file-driven.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Windrose Roadmap
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: What is confirmed for Windrose now, and what is planned before 1.0.
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Windrose does not use a date-by-date public roadmap yet, but the studio has already set the Early Access duration window, launch baseline scope, and clear feature priorities for near-term updates.

items.title: No date-by-date Windrose roadmap is published yet
items.summary: Kraken Express currently communicates through broad Early Access goals and priority areas rather than a milestone calendar with fixed dates.
highlights: Direction is public even without exact dates

items.title: Windrose pre-launch Early Access priorities were defined
items.summary: The studio outlined active priorities including co-op ship batteries, randomized loot, hold-to-gather, FOV slider, signal fires, DLSS or FSR support, controller improvements, dismantling workbench, and target-lock switching.

items.title: Windrose launched in Early Access with broad baseline content
items.summary: Launch scope included 3 biomes, around 30 procedural islands, more than 90 hand-crafted points of interest, 3 playable ships, naval combat with boarding, factions, reputation systems, NPC workers, and a long-form main story range.

items.title: Windrose expects roughly 1.5 to 2.5 years before full release
items.summary: The current Early Access plan indicates a long runway before 1.0, with continuous balancing, systems work, and content expansion during that period.

items.title: Windrose plans around 50 percent more full-version content
items.summary: The full release target includes major content growth beyond launch, with more biomes, bosses, enemies, ships, loot layers, and continued story progression.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Windrose Update Log
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.subtitle: A chronological Windrose feed of launch operations, hotfixes, and platform-impact changes.
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: The first Windrose update wave focused on launch operations, server connectivity, and save stability. This timeline tracks the announcements that changed real gameplay and hosting behavior.

items.title: 1,500,000 wishlists and pre-launch quality upgrades
items.summary: Kraken Express published a broad pre-launch improvement list shaped by demo feedback, including co-op expansion, loot randomization, gather-flow improvements, and graphics or input upgrades.

items.title: Windrose Early Access launch went live
items.summary: Windrose launched into Early Access on Steam, and the launch communication confirmed the studio rename from Windrose Crew to Kraken Express as full-version development began.

items.title: Hotfix 0.10.0.1.6
items.summary: The first visible post-launch hotfix cleaned up localization issues, corrected the Nitrado link, and changed server-info visibility in the pause menu to a safer default state.

items.title: Hotfix 0.10.0.2.54 with online downtime window
items.summary: This patch cycle removed dedicated-server token cooldown, added version mismatch warnings, moved invite codes to a 1-32 character limit, fixed ship-spawn crashes, corrected boss scaling for larger groups, increased host start timeout, and delivered broad combat and localization fixes.
highlights: Boss scaling fix for larger groups
Host start timeout raised to 5 minutes

items.title: Windrose Steam Cloud Saves update notice
items.summary: The team published official guidance for cloud-save instability, including single-device recommendations, manual backup workflow, and local restore instructions when corruption appears.
highlights: Client and dedicated save paths documented
Cloud behavior may be adjusted if issues persist

faq.title: Windrose Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything players ask most about Windrose right now.

questions.question: Is Windrose singleplayer or co-op?
questions.answer: Windrose supports solo play and online co-op. You can start alone and later host the same world for friends.

questions.question: How does Windrose multiplayer joining work?
questions.answer: Windrose uses invite-code joining through Connect to Server, with saved server access after the first connection.

questions.question: What should I prioritize in Windrose during the first hours?
questions.answer: Focus on coastal resource loops, a full starter camp, healing stock, and stamina-first combat habits before pushing harder zones.

questions.question: When did Windrose release in Early Access?
questions.answer: Windrose launched in Early Access on April 14, 2026, and continues receiving launch-window updates.

questions.question: Where can I play Windrose today?
questions.answer: Windrose is available on Steam and Epic Games Store, with official updates posted through the game site and Steam news.

questions.question: What is the current Windrose full-release window?
questions.answer: Current planning points to a late 2027 to late 2028 window for the Windrose 1.0 release cycle.

cta.title: Ready to Sail in Windrose?
cta.description: Join the official Windrose Discord to track patches, share builds, and stay updated with sea survival news.
